summary: binding.gyp uses GYP command-expansion to execute commands at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp at the tarball root whose fields contain GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(...)` / `<!@(...)` / `<@(...)`) at line 6 (within targets/sources). npm implicitly invokes `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a binding.gyp is present — even without any declared install/postinstall lifecycle script — and GYP evaluates command-expansion as a shell command during its configure step. This causes the embedded command to run automatically on `npm install`, functionally equivalent to a lifecycle hook. The package's stated purpose is a Backstage GitLab backend plugin (pure TypeScript/JavaScript); it does not require a native addon, and there is no legitimate reason for a binding.gyp to live in this package, let alone one carrying command-expansion. This shape executes attacker-controlled code on the installer's machine at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
